description:
You are acting as a build-engineer who has just received a fresh set of JAR artifacts
    from the nightly pipeline.  
    The artifacts have already been copied to:

        /home/user/builds/artifacts

    Current contents of that directory (before you start):

        app-core-1.2.0.jar
        app-core-1.2.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
        app-ui-1.2.0.jar

    Goal
    ----
    1. Create a **text file** called  

           /home/user/builds/release-summary.log  

       that lists a one-line summary for every *stable* (i.e. **without the string
       “SNAPSHOT” in the file-name**) JAR.

    2. Every line of the summary **must follow this exact format** (no spaces, no
       trailing characters):

           <artifact_filename>|<size_in_bytes>

       • <artifact_filename> – the file name exactly as it appears in the directory  
       • <size_in_bytes>   – the file’s size in raw bytes, obtained with any standard
         Linux utility (e.g., stat ‑c%s …).  
       • Lines must be terminated with a single Unix newline (LF, “\n”).  
       • The file must contain **only** these lines—no header, footer or blank lines.

    3. The lines inside /home/user/builds/release-summary.log **must be sorted in
       ascending lexicographic order** by <artifact_filename>.

    4. Once the file has been created, print its contents to the terminal so the user
       can immediately verify it on-screen.

    Constraints & Hints
    -------------------
    • Ignore every JAR whose name contains the substring “SNAPSHOT”.  
    • Use any standard GNU/Linux text-processing utilities you like (find, grep,
      sort, awk, sed, printf, etc.); the entire task can be completed with three or
      four short commands.  
    • Do **not** rename, delete or modify the original JAR files.  
    • Make sure /home/user/builds/release-summary.log is overwritten if it already
      exists so the summary always reflects the latest set of artifacts.  

    Automated testing will:
      1. Check that /home/user/builds/release-summary.log exists.
      2. Verify that its exact byte-for-byte contents match the required format and
         values, including correct file sizes and ordering.
truth:
<!--
        PRE-POPULATED SYSTEM STATE (before the agent starts)
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/builds/artifacts/app-core-1.2.0.jar">CORE_1.2.0_CONTENT\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/builds/artifacts/app-core-1.2.1-SNAPSHOT.jar">CORE_1.2.1_SNAPSHOT_CONTENT\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/builds/artifacts/app-ui-1.2.0.jar">UI_1.2.0_CONTENT\n</file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        EXACT CONTENT EXPECTED **AFTER** SUCCESSFUL EXECUTION
    -->
    <expected_output_file path="/home/user/builds/release-summary.log"><![CDATA[
app-core-1.2.0.jar|19
app-ui-1.2.0.jar|17
]]></expected_output_file>
